IIT Delhi Latest Updates and News

30 Jun, 2026 IIT Delhi JEE Advanced Cutoff 2026 has been released. Check round and course-wise IIT Delhi JEE Advanced Cutoff 2026.

  • Round 2 Cutoff for B.Tech is 123-12244 for the General Category.

29 Jun, 2026 IIT JAM JOAPS 2026 is ongoing @jam2026.iitb.ac.in. Check latest updates below:

  • Fourth Allotment List is out. Check Allotment. Shortlisted candidates must pay the seat booking fee by June 30. Additional round allotment to be out on July 3, 2026. Read More.
  • Withdrawal option is available till June 30, 2026. Read News.

IIT Delhi IIT JAM Cutoff 2026 has been released for the M.Sc program. Round 3 Cutoff Rank for the General Category is 11-627. Check IIT Delhi IIT JAM 2026 Cutoff.

...Read More

IIT Delhi cutoff is available for JEE-Advanced, GATE, IIT-JAM, CAT, UCEED, CEED here. You can check category wise IIT Delhi cutoff for popular courses such as B.Tech, B.Tech + M.Tech, BS, M.S, M.Tech, M.Sc, MBA, B.Des, M.Des below.

IIT Delhi offers admission to B.Tech through JEE-Advanced. For Admission in B.Tech + M.Tech courses, IIT Delhi accept JEE-Advanced score. You can check the table below to know the accepted exams for courses offered by IIT Delhi (Cutoff Range below is across all categories).

Course Exam Cutoff
B.Tech JEE-Advanced 3 - 12244
B.Tech + M.Tech JEE-Advanced 1 - 3779
BS JEE-Advanced 520 - 8430
M.S GATE 300 - 525
M.Tech GATE 300 - 780
M.Sc IIT-JAM 7 - 5095
MBA CAT 60 - 98
B.Des UCEED 1 - 32
M.Des CEED 32 - 55

IIT Delhi JEE-Advanced Cutoff 2026

Students can take admission in B.Tech, B.Tech + M.Tech, BS programs at IIT Delhi based on JEE-Advanced score. As per IIT Delhi JEE-Advanced Cutoff 2026, the overall cutoff for B.Tech programs in Round 2 is 3 - 12244 across all categories.

  • For B.Tech + M.Tech, The Overall closing rank in 2026 in Round 2 is 1 - 3779 across all categories.
  • For BS, The Overall closing rank in 2026 in Round 2 is 520 - 8430 across all categories.

B.Tech Computer Science and Engineering, B.Tech + M.Tech Computer Science & Engineering, BS Chemistry, are the top choices for students appearing in JEE-Advanced.

In 2026, B.Tech Computer Science and Engineering closing rank for General Category is 123 in Round 2.

  • For B.Tech + M.Tech Computer Science & Engineering, The JEE-Advanced closing rank 2026 for General is 211 in Round 2.
  • For BS Chemistry, The JEE-Advanced closing rank 2026 for General is 8228 - 8430 in Round 2.

You can check B.Tech Computer Science and Engineering, B.Tech + M.Tech Computer Science & Engineering, BS Chemistry, category-wise closing rank for JEE-Advanced below:

IIT Delhi JEE-Advanced Cutoff Trends

Admission at IIT Delhi in B.Tech Programs in 2026 was closed at 12244 rank whereas closing rank in 2025 was 12217 across all categories.

  • Admission in B.Tech + M.Tech Programs in 2026 was closed at 3779 rank whereas closing rank in 2025 was 3454 across all categories.
  • Admission in BS Programs in 2026 was closed at 8430 rank whereas closing rank in 2025 was 8528 across all categories.

IIT Delhi GATE Cutoff 2026

Students can take admission in M.S, M.Tech programs at IIT Delhi based on GATE score. As per IIT Delhi GATE Cutoff 2026, the overall cutoff for M.S programs in Round 1 is 300 - 525 across all categories.

  • For M.Tech, The Overall closing marks in 2026 in Round 1 is 300 - 780 across all categories.

M.S Electrical Engineering, M.Tech Computer Science And Engineering, are the top choices for students appearing in GATE.

In 2026, M.S Electrical Engineering closing marks for General Category is 370 in Round 1.

  • For M.Tech Computer Science And Engineering, The GATE closing marks 2026 for General is 780 in Round 1.

Cutoff Summary: IIT Delhi

  • GATE
  • JEE Advanced
  • IIT JAM
  • UCEED

The GATE 2025 last round cutoff for admission to M.Tech programs at IIT Delhi in the General category ranges from a score of 800 to 425. For the M.Tech Computer Science and Engineering program, the admission cutoff is 800, while the last round cutoff for M.Tech Data Science is 720. Other sought-after courses such as Cyber Security, Structural Engineering, and Radio Frequency Design and Technology show admission cutoffs in the 710 to 630 range.

Courses like Biomedical Engineering, Electric Mobility, and Instrumentation have cutoffs between 450 and 425. For OBC candidates, the admission cutoff score is 720 for Computer Science and Engineering and 375 for Instrumentation. In the SC category, the last round cutoff score ranges from 550 for Computer Science and Engineering to 300 for Water Resources Engineering. For ST applicants, the admission cutoff is 640 for Radio Frequency Design and Technology and 300 for Data Science.

Under the EWS category, the last round cutoff is 800 for Computer Science and Engineering and 425 for Electric Mobility.

 By Collegedunia’s Personal AI

IIT Delhi Important Events

M.Sc2

Can I get IIT Delhi with 4000 rank in JEE Advanced?

Kapil Bahl

4000 JEE Advanced rank and IIT Delhi — nuanced answer:

at 4000 general category rank, IIT Delhi mainstream branches are closed:

  • CSE, ECE, EE, Mech, Chemical, Civil at IIT Delhi — all close in the range of 50–600 for general. 4000 rank won't get these
  • what might open at 4000 at IIT Delhi: Maths & Computing, Engineering Physics, Textile Technology, Production & Industrial Engineering — these close in the 3,000–5,000 range some years. marginal, check latest JoSAA data

better targets at 4000 general JEE Advanced:

  • IIT Roorkee — Chemical, Civil Engineering likely
  • IIT Guwahati — ECE, EE, Chemical might open
  • IIT BHU (Varanasi) — CSE, ECE, EE available
  • IIT Dhanbad/ISM — CSE, ECE
  • Newer IITs — CSE/ECE easily

check the exact closing ranks on josaa.admissions.nic.in for JoSAA 2024 — the Maths & Computing program at IIT Delhi is your best shot. if interdisciplinary programs interest you (like Maths & Computing), it's worth applying. if you want core engineering branches, focus on IIT Roorkee/Guwahati/BHU at 4000 general.

What is the JEE Advanced cutoff 2024 for IIT Delhi?

ShwetaB.Tech student at IIT Delhi

The JEE Advanced cutoff 2024 is not yet released for IIT Delhi. 

But here is the cutoff 2023 for admission into various B.Tech branches at IIT Delhi for your reference-

IIT Delhi B.Tech cutoff 2023 

Branch Closing Rank (Round 6)
Biotechnology and Biochemical Engineering 5687
Chemical Engineering 2365
Civil Engineering 4472
Computer Science and Engineering 118
Electrical Engineering 582
Electrical Engineering (Power and Automation) 828
Energy Engineering 2910
Engineering and Computational Mechanics 1255
Engineering Physics 2783
Materials Engineering 4055
Mathematics and Computing 352
Mechanical Engineering 1791
Production and Industrial Engineering 3273
Textile Technology 6690

I have only mentioned the cutoffs for open category and gender-neutral seats. 

For more information on the IIT Delhi B.Tech cutoff 2023 for various categories, you can visit the official JoSAA website- https://josaa.admissions.nic.in/applicant/SeatAllotmentResult/CurrentORCR.aspx

Which IIT Delhi B tech branch will I get under 500 jee advanced rank?

Shiddhi Singh

Candidates who score 500th rank in JEE Advanced stand a decent chance of receiving admission to the BTech and Integrated BTech programs at IIT Delhi. Applicants can easily be into IIT Delhi if their cutoff ranks fall between 102 and 574. The IIT Delhi JEE Advanced cutoffs with a rank of 500 or lower are listed below for the different branches:

Courses

185

180

BTech Mathematics and Computing

306

BTech + MTech Mathematics and Computing

What is the minimum rank to get into IIT Delhi?

Kunal SharmaB.Tech. from Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi

Different branches have different opening ranks and one needs to clear the cutoff to secure a seat in that specific branch. JEE Cutoff 2023 will be decided on the following parameters 

  • Previous year’s cutoff trends
  • The number of candidates who appeared for the exam
  • Availability of seats at IIT Delhi
  • The difficulty level of JEE Advanced 2023
  • Category of Candidates

Since JoSAA hasn’t yet revealed the Cutoff for this year, let’s consider the previous year's cutoff to get an idea of the rank required to secure admission to IIT Delhi.

IIT Delhi JEE Cutoff 2022

What is the CGPA required for changing branch (textile to any other) in IIT Delhi?

Kunal SharmaB.Tech. from Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi

A UG student at IIT Delhi is eligible for branch change only at the end of the first year subject to the following criteria

  • CGPA for General and OBC category: >8.00
  • CGPA for SC/ST and PwD category: >7.00
  • The student must have earned credits /non-graded units at the end of the second semester of the first year.
  • Additionally, the student should have no disciplinary action against him/her.
  • The change of branch is done solely on merit under the condition that the batch size of the branch to which the transfer is to be made should not exceed its permitted limit by more than 15% and the batch size of the branch from which the transfer is being made does not fall below 85% of its permitted limit.
  • In case of a tie of CGPA between the students applying for branch change, the JEE Ranks of the students will be given due consideration.

These are some of the basic conditions under which branch change is made in IIT Delhi.

0
